Thu, Mar 13, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Create26

#7 Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Hiflylabs .

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9
54 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: Hiflylabs
🏠Venue Host: Create 26; 1061 Budapest, Király Street 26.
🍕Catering: Snacks & beers

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

6:00 - 6:30 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
6:30 - 6:35 | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
6:35 - 6:55 | Presentation 1 (15-20 min)
6:55 - 7:00 | Presentation 1 Q&A (5 min)
7:00 - 7:20 | Presentation 2 (15-20 min)
7:20 - 7:25 | Presentation 2 Q&A (5 min)
7:25 - 7:30 | Closing Remarks (5 min)
7:30 - 9:00 | Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks

🗣️Presentation #1: DuckDB: Run dbt build with sub-second execution times

Description:

We'll explore the fundamentals of DuckDB, including its internal architecture, engine design, and "friendly" SQL dialect, while learning about its most impactful use cases — like powering dbt pipelines. You'll discover what makes DuckDB different from traditional databases, including its in-process design and lightweight, dependency-free codebase. Whether you're new to DuckDB or looking to optimize your workflows, this session will provide practical insights to help you leverage its full potential.

Speaker bio:

Gábor Szárnyas, Developer Relations Advocate and Technical Writer at DuckDB Labs

(Talk in English 🇺🇸)

----

🗣️Presentation #2: Avoid the Avalanche: Practical dbt tips for your Snowflake workflows

Description:

Using dbt with Snowflake is a powerful combination, but you may also find yourself facing unexpected challenges. In this talk, we'll explore practical tips and tricks for dbt development on Snowflake while covering key concepts like materializations, pricing, warehouse sizing, and clustering. You'll see how a few thoughtful adjustments can significantly boost both efficiency and performance. Whether you're new to Snowflake or already have some experience, this talk aims to provide practical insights you can apply directly to your everyday workflows.

Speaker bio:

Tibor Kusztós is an Analytics Engineer at Hiflylabs

(Talk in English 🇺🇸)

EVENT DETAILS:

The doors open at 6:00pm. Presentations begin at 6:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.
Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and and reach out to marketing@hiflylabs.com if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-\ channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 8:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:
Skip to content

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9
54 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: Create 26; 1061 Budapest, Király utca 26.
🍕Catering: snacks and beers
🤝Organizer: Hiflylabs

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

6:30 - 7:00 | Registration (30 min)
7:00 - 7:05 | Welcome Remarks (5 min)
7:05 - 7:25 | Talk 1 (15-20 min)
7:25 - 7:30 | Q&A (5 min)
7:30 - 7:50 | Talk 2 (15-20 min)
7:50 - 7:55 | Q&A (5 min)
7:55 - 8:00 | Closing Remarks; dbt slides (5 min)
8:00 - 9:00 | Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks!

🗣️Presentation #1:
Switching the query engine under a dbt project - Lessons from the migration trenches

Description:
Transitioning a dbt project isn't as easy as flipping a switch, but it can be done smoothly under the right conditions. Our first speaker will share the adventures of moving dbt projects across platforms like Redshift, Snowflake, and Databricks. You'll discover key principles for future migrations and gain practical advice on how to organize your dbt project to stay flexible for whatever comes next.

Speaker bio:
Laci Pataki - Senior Data and Analytics Engineer

(Talk in English 🇺🇸)

---

🗣️Presentation #2:
How to supercharge your dbt Testing - Best Practices & Pitfalls

Description:
Testing is essential for gaining user trust, yet it has traditionally been limited (or fully absent) in SQL workflows. Fortunately, dbt provides a robust set of tools to test dbt projects. In this talk, we will cover essential tips and tricks for testing your dbt project, explore various types of tests, and discuss common pitfalls to avoid.

Speaker bio:
Juan Perafan – Analytics Engineer consultant at Xebia, co-author of the book "Fundamentals of Analytics Engineering, host of the "SQL Lingua Franca" podcast series

(Talk in English 🇺🇸)

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 6:30pm. Presentations begin at 7:00pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.
Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and and reach out to marketing@hiflylabs.com if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-budapest channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: Create 26; 1061 Király utca 26.
🍕Catering: snacks and beers
🤝Organizer: Hiflylabs

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://bit.ly/4azcreT

📝Agenda

6:00 - 6:30 | Registration (30 min)
6:30 - 6:35 | Welcome Remarks (5 min)
6:35 - 6:55 | Talk 1 (15-20 min)
6:55 - 7:00 | Q&A (5 min)
7:00 - 7:20 | Talk 2 (15-20 min)
7:20 - 7:25 | Q&A (5 min)
7:25 - 7:30 | Closing Remarks; dbt slides (5 min)
7:30 - 8:30 | Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks!

🗣️Presentation #1:
Data Vault + dbt: What makes this combo work?

Description:
Data Vault: What is it, when to use it and how to implement it in dbt? The Data Vault has been kicking around for over a decade, but clarity on its use and benefits is still not common. Time to turn on the light. Our first speaker goes into the what, why, and how of Data Vault, especially its potential with dbt. If your data warehouse is more maze than model, stick around.

Speaker bio:
Balázs Ragályi – Senior Data and Analytics Engineer, Data Vault 2.0 expert

(Talk in English 🇺🇸)

---

🗣️Presentation #2:
Journey into the Cosmos: Integration of dbt and Apache Airflow

Description:
Data orchestration and transformation are key components in the modern data stack, and tools like Apache Airflow and dbt play a crucial role in our workflows. This session will be about the Cosmos project—an open-source solution that simplifies the integration of Apache Airflow and dbt. Actively contributing to the Cosmos project, our second speaker has a unique perspective, and is excited to guide you through the synergy of two robust tools.

Speaker bio:
Péter Szécsi – Data Architect, Open Source Contributor, Kaggle Competitions Expert

(Talk in Hungarian 🇭🇺 with English slides)

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 6pm. Presentations begin at 6:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.
Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and and reach out to marketing@hiflylabs.com if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-budapest channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt allows teams to ship trusted data products, faster. dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Király utca 26 · Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Hiflylabs .

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9

Details

We're excited to host the 4th Budapest dbt Meetup! All data enthusiasts » Let's shape Hungarian dbt community, enjoy talks, snack & beer together!

We will be in-person in Spot Budapest, which is an exciting location in the stunning neighbourhood of the famous Budapest downtown.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: Hiflylabs

🏠Venue Host: The Spot Budapest, 26 Király Street, Budapest 1061

https://create26.hu/

🍕Catering: beer&snacks

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

6:00 - 6:30 | Registration (30 min)

6:30 - 6:35 | Welcome Remarks (5 min)

6:35 - 6:55 | Talk 1 (15-20 min)

6:55 - 7:00 | Q&A (5 min)

7:00 - 7:20 | Talk 2 (15-20 min)

7:20 - 7:25 | Q&A (5 min)

7:25 - 7:30 | Closing Remarks (5 min)

7:30 - 8:30 | Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks!

🗣️Presentation #1: How Python on Databricks gives antigravity to dbt?

Zsombor will delve into dbt's second language: Python. Using Databricks as a warehouse, he'll illustrate the capabilities of dbt's Python models. The presentation will introduce how Python enhances dbt's traditional SQL-based function set, highlighting use cases from the practical to the cutting-edge theory of Python models.

Speaker: Zsombor Földesi, Lead Data Engineer, Hiflylabs

🗣️Presentation #2: Do you really need real-time data?

Real-time analytics can be useful, but building and maintaining real-time ETL pipelines is often time-consuming. For many scenarios, a batch pipeline implemented using the modern data stack can accomplish the same goal. In this presentation, Ben will share how we build batch and real-time pipelines and how we decide which one is appropriate for different scenarios.

Speaker: Ben Kulcsar, Senior Data Engineer, Hotjar

The language of the presentations is English.

The doors open at 6pm. Presentations begin at 6:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-budapest channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

----------------------------------

dbt is an open source command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

events.raw_text:

Thu, Nov 10, 2022 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM CET

The Spot Budapest

Király utca 26 · Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Dóra R.

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9
54 reviews
Details

We're excited to host the 2nd Budapest dbt Meetup! All data enthusiasts » Let's shape Hungarian dbt community, enjoy talks, snack & beer together!
We will be in-person in Hubhub, which is an exciting location in the stunning neighbourhood of the famous Budapest downtown.

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🤝Organizer: Hiflylabs
🏠Venue Host: The Spot Budapest (Hubhub) 26 Király Street, Budapest 1061
https://www.thespotcowork.com/hu/budapest/
🍕Catering: beer&snacks

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda

6:00 - 6:30 | Registration (30 min)
6:30 - 6:35 | Welcome Remarks (5 min)
6:35 - 6:55 | Talk 1 (15-20 min)
6:55 - 7:00 | Q&A (5 min)
7:00 - 7:20 | Talk 2 (15-20 min)
7:20 - 7:25 | Q&A (5 min)
7:25 - 7:30 | Closing Remarks (5 min)
7:30 - 8:30 | Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks!

🗣️Presentation #1: From Stored Procedures to dbt:
In this presentation, Zsombor will talk about the process of migrating existing on-premise DWHs to the Cloud while adopting dbt as well. He will cover the technical aspects of the process, shares Hiflylabs' best practices of refactoring legacy SQL codes to dbt.
The language of the presentation is Hungarian.

Speaker: Zsombor Földesi, Senior Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs

---

🗣️Presentation #2: BI as code: How to 10x your productivity as an analytics engineer
Analytics engineers have the same needs as software engineers and there’s an increasing number of developer tools appearing with a focus on analytics engineers. In this talk we’re going to focus on some of the latest developer tooling for BI and data, including automating deployments, preview environments, and generating code for BI.
The language of the presentation is English.

Speaker: Oliver Laslett, Co-Founder & CTO, Lightdash

The doors open at 6pm. Presentations begin at 6:30pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.
➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-budapest channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is an open source command-line tool that speaks the preferred language of data analysts everywhere—SQL. With dbt, analysts take ownership of the entire analytics engineering workflow, from writing data transformation code to deployment and documentation.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Coalesce: The Analytics Engineering Conference
Network event

D

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9

Details

In case you missed it, dbt Labs is hosting Coalesce 2022 from October 17 - 21. It is the annual conference dedicated to advancing the practice of analytics engineering. Coalesce offers data practitioners an opportunity to deepen their knowledge and network. You can choose your own adventure including in-person, online, and hybrid experiences.

Coalesce Online (FREE) | October 17-21, 2022. The online experience that you know and love. Connect with data practitioners around the globe in sessions designed for North America, APAC, EMEA and LATAM regions. Catch live stream sessions from Coalesce New Orleans, interact with the community and speakers via Slack, and attend exclusively virtual sessions.
Coalesce New Orleans | October 17-21, 2022 | Located at the Hyatt Regency, New Orleans. Our first time together, in person! Attend a presentation, workshop, or panel discussion. Participate in activities, a hackathon, intimate discussions, and social activities. Meet community members and partners while exploring the history and culture of New Orleans.
Coalesce Online + | London, 20 October 2022 | Sydney, 21 October 2022. A one-day event taking place in London and Sydney, complementing Coalesce Online. This will include tech talks, hosted discussions, an Executive keynote and a post-event networking session.

⚠️Please note that Coalesce 2022 won't be hosted on Meetup.com.
✅REGISTER + SIGN UP for sessions here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com
📅Review the schedule here: https://coalesce.getdbt.com/schedule
⭐As a member of the dbt Meetup Network, please use your discount code MEETUP15 for Premium and GA tickets.

Tue, May 10, 2022 · 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM CEST

The Spot Budapest

Király utca 26 · Budapest

Budapest dbt Meetup (first edition)
D

Hosted by dbt L. and 2 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Budapest dbt Meetup
4.9
54 reviews
Details

This event is jointly organized with the Budapest Big Data & Analytics Meetup group.

We're excited to host our 1st dbt Meetup in Budapest! Let's shape Hungarian dbt history together on 10 May!

We will be in-person in Hubhub, which is an exciting location in the stunning neighbourhood of the famous Budapest downtown! If you rather give up beer and snacks, but you wouldn't miss the experience, join our livestream available at:[ https://bit.ly/3iO6Nvu](https://bit.ly/3iO6Nvu)

Organizer: Hiflylabs

Venue: Hubhub 26 Király Street, Budapest 1061 https://www.hubhub.com/en/budapest-kiraly26/

Catering: beer&snacks

Agenda:
6:30 pm: Talk 1
6:50 pm: Q&A
7:00 pm: Talk 2
7:20 pm: Q&A
7:30 pm: Networking - let's have a chat, beer and snacks!

Join the #local-budapest channel in dbt Slack! This is where the chat will take place during meetups. Receive an invite to join the dbt Slack community:
https://community.getdbt.com/

Speakers:
🎤Andras Zimmer CTO, Hiflylabs
Two-week dev cycles in a DW -- agility is reality with open source:
In this presentation we first talk briefly about what dbt is. Then we pick situations that make the traditional data warehouse / data analysis workflows painfully difficult, and show how dbt and its ecosystem alleviates these. We cover some technical aspects as well as a few communication and work organization related ones.
The language of the presentation is Hungarian.

🎤 Mark Rittman CEO, Rittman Analytics & Son N. Nguyen, Analytics Engineer, Hiflylabs
Super-performant interactive dashboards on huge datasets with dbt, Firebolt and Looker:
Firebolt is a new data warehousing technology that promises sub-second response times on very large datasets with highly-concurrent workloads - think of it as "speed of Clickhouse meets Snowflake architecture". In this presentation we'll explain how Firebolt works and how we used dbt to load and transform data from the Hacker News public dataset to create a fast, interactive Looker dashboard.
The language of the presentation is English.

COVID Policy:
Any public space where other people are present holds an inherent risk of exposure to COVID-19 and other communicable diseases. By attending this event, I agree to voluntarily assume all risk related to exposure and agree to not hold dbt Labs or any of their affiliates including partners and sponsors, directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, volunteers (“Representatives”) or venues liable for illness. I understand and agree that dbt Labs has an interest and obligation in protecting the health and well-being of dbt Labs Representatives, venue staff and other attendees; therefore, I agree that, if I’ve chosen not to be fully vaccinated as determined by my local country’s public health guidelines for personal, religious, medical or other reasons, I will only participate in a dbt Labs meetup remotely by watching live or via a recording, and I will not attend in-person. When in attendance in person, I will take necessary precautions while at the event in accordance with local and national health guidelines and regulations, which may include, but be not limited to, engaging in appropriate social distancing, wearing a mask in public areas when not consuming food or beverages, minimizing face touching, frequently washing hands and avoiding risky environments such as overcrowded bars or restaurants. I agree to not attend any dbt Labs event if I feel ill or have had recent exposure to a potentially COVID-19 infected individual. Venue may have other requirements in place, and I agree to abide by those requirements when disclosed by venue.
